title: Help
subtitle: A detailed guide to the main OpenCairn workflows: adding sources, editing notes, reviewing AI work, managing projects, and collaborating with a team.

quick.title: Guided workflows
quick.subtitle: Start from the job you are trying to do instead of memorizing feature names.

steps.1: Open a project or create a new one.
steps.2: Choose file, link, or text from Add source depending on the material you have.
steps.3: Use the analysis tab to follow parsing, OCR, extraction, AI analysis, and persistence status.
steps.4: Open the original source, artifacts, and generated notes from the left project tree to review the result.

steps.1: Prepare the project and key notes that should feed the report.
steps.2: Request document generation from the context panel or project tools.
steps.3: Confirm the template, output format, and source scope before starting the job.
steps.4: Review results, warnings, downloads, and provider exports from Workflow Console and the generated-file viewer.

steps.1: Check members and roles from workspace settings.
steps.2: Keep the project and notes inside the same work boundary, then invite the right people.
steps.3: Leave review points through note comments and mentions.
steps.4: Use notifications, share links, and permissions to separate internal collaboration from external sharing.

steps.1: Confirm project sources have been analyzed and notes or graph data exist.
steps.2: Inspect concept connections and weak evidence in the graph first.
steps.3: Open flashcards or question flows from the learning tools.
steps.4: For weak concepts, return to the related notes, add source material, or ask AI for an evidence-grounded explanation.

workspace.title: Choose a workspace
workspace.body: A workspace is the top-level boundary for sources, projects, members, and permissions. Use the lower-left menu to confirm the current space or switch to another one.

project.title: Create a project
project.body: A project groups related sources and notes. The project screen brings together notes, graph, learning, agents, and document-generation workflows.

firstSource.title: Add the first source
firstSource.body: Start an empty project by uploading a file, importing a link, or pasting text. Once analysis starts, the progress tab shows each processing stage.

tabs.title: Keep work in tabs
tabs.body: Dashboard, projects, notes, import, help, report issue, and settings open in the same editor tab flow so you can keep context without leaving the workspace.

upload.title: Upload files
upload.body: Upload PDFs and documents into a project, then track parsing, OCR, Markdown splitting, image/table extraction, AI analysis, and project-tree persistence.

link.title: Import web links
link.body: Register http or https links as project sources. Link sources are attached to the project and can later participate in search and AI context.

text.title: Paste text
text.body: For quick notes, meeting records, and drafts that do not exist as files, paste text directly and turn it into a note immediately.

imports.title: External imports
imports.body: Markdown ZIP, Notion ZIP, and Google Drive import paths live under the import tab's additional options. Long-running jobs expose separate status and failure details.

notes.title: Edit notes
notes.body: Notes open in the collaborative editor. Titles, body text, links, tables, code, quotes, and embeds are handled in one knowledge-document surface.

reading.title: Reading mode
reading.body: Switch a note tab into reading mode when you want to review content with less editing chrome.

source.title: Source view
source.body: For notes with original material, source view lets you inspect the source document and compare it with the generated note.

canvas.title: Canvas and execution output
canvas.body: When a source needs code or visualization, the canvas flow shows generated output. Browser execution runs inside an isolated environment.

chat.title: Context panel
chat.body: The right panel is where you ask questions, request summaries, generate outputs, and check work status using the current workspace, project, and note context.

scope.title: Control evidence scope
scope.body: AI answers are grounded in project sources and retrieval evidence. Scope chips and retrieval modes let you choose narrower or expanded evidence.

documents.title: Generate documents
documents.body: Ask for reports, technical documents, business documents, images, PDF, DOCX, PPTX, or XLSX outputs. Results appear as project files and workflow-console runs.

review.title: Review before applying
review.body: Write-capable actions such as note updates, code patches, installs, previews, and command runs are designed around review cards and explicit approval.

tree.title: Project tree
tree.body: Open folders, notes, uploaded sources, analysis artifacts, generated files, and code workspaces from one tree. Start here when you need to find a project object.

graph.title: Knowledge graph
graph.body: Inspect connections between notes and concepts. Weak or missing evidence is shown as status so you can find what needs follow-up.

learning.title: Learning tools
learning.body: Use flashcards, Socratic prompts, and score views to check understanding from the material in a project.

files.title: Generated files
files.body: AI-generated documents and code outputs are stored as project objects instead of chat-only artifacts. Open them in viewers, download them, or inspect provider export status.

members.title: Members and roles
members.body: Manage workspace members, invites, and roles from settings. Permissions flow from workspace to project to page.

comments.title: Comments and mentions
comments.body: Leave comments inside notes and mention teammates. The collaboration server syncs simultaneous note editing.

sharing.title: Share links
sharing.body: When a note needs external access, use a share link with a separated public surface instead of exposing the full workspace.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.body: Collaboration events such as comments, mentions, and invites follow notification and email preferences. Use the lower-left notification button to review them.

workflow.title: Workflow console
workflow.body: Import, document generation, code execution, preview, and agent jobs expose status and logs through the workflow console.

agents.title: Project agents
agents.body: Project agents such as Librarian, Synthesis, Curator, Connector, Staleness, and Narrator help organize sources, suggest connections, check stale content, and create audio outputs.

code.title: Code workspaces
code.body: Generated code is stored in a separate workspace and handled through patch review, command runs, install approvals, and static preview steps.

exports.title: Exports
exports.body: Generated documents can be downloaded as files or exported to connected providers such as Google Workspace when that integration is available.

profile.title: Profile and language
profile.body: Update name, display language, region, and theme from the personal settings area. Korean is the default locale and English is also supported.

providers.title: AI providers
providers.body: Gemini, Ollama, and OpenAI-compatible provider paths depend on deployment settings and whether a personal key is registered.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.body: Check Google Drive, MCP server, and external export connection status from account or workspace settings.

billing.title: Plan and usage
billing.body: Self-hosted and public-repository functionality are separate from hosted-service plans. Hosted billing and usage appear only when the operator enables them.

upload.title: Upload or analysis is stuck
upload.body: Check the import tab, ingest progress tab, then workflow console. These surfaces show the failed stage, error message, and whether retry is available.

evidence.title: AI evidence is weak
evidence.body: Confirm the project has enough source material, related notes are in the same project, and graph relationships are not missing.

permissions.title: Permission errors
permissions.body: Confirm you are in the right workspace and project, then check your role in the members settings tab. Ask an admin to adjust access if needed.

report.title: Send a bug or feedback report
report.body: Use the report issue tab to send type, priority, title, and details. The operator inbox also receives the current page and browser metadata.
